properties.name: DANO 100 - Mjölkudden Raststuga Vindskydd
properties.desc:
Very nice place. Lovely sandy beach Shelter for 5 people and cabin with 4 beds Lots of wood but no axe In nature reserves with magnificent hiking trails A blocked forest road leads all the way. 1,6 km easy hiking To use this shelter you need to buy a Nature Conservation Card. These cards pay for the expansion of campsites, maintenance, wood, supervision, waste containers, nature conservation and compensation to landowners who have granted the use of their land for campsites. Nature Conservation Cards can be purchased online, at the tourist offices in the area or from local canoe rentals. The card costs SEK 60 per person and night. Children under 12 use the service free of charge. Buy Online: https://dalslandnordmarken.se/en/shop/

properties.name: Fjärdbotten Stuga
properties.desc:
Fjärdbottens fäbodvall consists of three timbered cottages with two sleeping breezes each and a larger timber building of approximately 35 square meters with long tables and benches. It features barbecue facilities, dry toilet and a 15-seat guest jetty. In total, the embankment can accommodate about 25 people under roof. Camping is also possible. The embankment is always open. No advance booking is possible. Adjacent to the embankment there are two reliable cold springs.

A toilet is available and the water right next to the house is supposed to be drinking water (told by a boat owner). The text on the map also indicates a shower.

properties.name: Mangslidberget brandtornet
properties.desc:
The tower is a metal structure, about 15 metres high. At the top of the tower there is a cabin with room for 2 sleepers. Also available are many facilities such as a heater and a kerosene lamp with kerosene. Really a cool place to spend the night. The steps upwards are very scary, so not for scared people. We ourselves had coffee and a meal here, but because we couldn't lift our dog upwards, we unfortunately didn't sleep here.

Object Type: Tower

properties.name: Stora Bollemyr Stuga
properties.desc:
Längs led: Soteleden 
Totalbetyg (1-10): 9
Ved: Nej 
Grillplats: Öppen eld, finns galler. 
Bänkar/Bord (platser): Ja, mycket fint med grova bjälkar runt eldplatsen. 
Antal personer (sovande i skydd): 2
Liten stuga i mycket fint skick, ligger fint med berg bakom och myrvatten framför. Värt att veta är att stugan är väldigt liten och har två längsgående bänkar inne. Dessa sov vi på, men de är inte mer än ca 40 cm breda... Så för oss två smala kvinnor på 168 cm gick det utmärkt - men andra kan få problem. 
Ingen ved och inget dass.
Obs! Myren har öppet vatten, men inte mycket eller djupt. Vi använde vattnet till matlagning och te/kaffe - men kokade först.

properties.name: Storön, raststuga
properties.desc:
On an island in the lake Kläggen Wood: no split wood, but many cubes laid out at the back and in a small wood store. In addition, many windfalls partially cut up to cubes, saw and axe are missing however Barbecue facilities: yes, outdoor, grills available. In the cottage there was a small iron stove Toilet: yes, one locked and one open between the 1st shelter and the cottage, about 60m away Sleeping area: floor 7x5m + bunk bed (2-?(many!) There were bins in several places on the island but I don't know how it works with emptying out there. It is supposed to be the village team that looks after the island, I was told by acquaintances. Connection: by own boat, 900m from public bathing area.

properties.desc:
The area is refurbished and nice, located along a part of the upland trail called church path. The church path seems to have received a lot of attention, it was in good condition. There is a road all the way up, easy to par a car or bike. In the charcoal kiln it was cramped and dark, if you lie under the table it might be possible to sleep there. Outside there is a fireplace, firewood and toilet, and a cold spring with good water. There is space to pitch a tent.

properties.name: Kolebacka Stuga
properties.desc:
A small cottage with one larger and one smaller room, fireplace outside, bench table No water, no toilet, no firewood. Located along Bohusleden stage 7 and some other marked trails. According to the chairman of the Nature Conservation Association in Kungälv, whom I met on site, the cabin is always open. There is an option to lock, but he has not seen it locked at any time. Located in a nature reserve. https://www.kungalv.se/kultur--fritid/utomhusaktiviteter/parker/fontins-naturreservat/

properties.name: DANO 7 - Guppviksön Vindskydd
properties.desc:
To use this shelter you need to buy a Nature Conservation Card. These cards pay for the expansion of campsites, maintenance, wood, supervision, waste containers, nature conservation and compensation to landowners who have granted the use of their land for campsites. Nature Conservation Cards can be purchased online, at the tourist offices in the area or from local canoe rentals. The card costs SEK 60 per person and night. Children under 12 use the service free of charge. Buy Online: https://dalslandnordmarken.se/en/shop/

Nestled in the heart of the serene Swedish wilderness of Gävleborg County, this rustic sleeping shelter, or *wiata*, offers an idyllic retreat for nature lovers seeking tranquility and adventure. Surrounded by towering pine and birch trees, the wiata stands as a testament to simple, functional design, perfectly harmonizing with its picturesque lakeside location. The shelter is thoughtfully constructed from sturdy wooden planks and beams, with an open front that provides breathtaking, uninterrupted views of the shimmering lake and undulating forested landscapes beyond. Inside, there is ample space for several sleeping bags and hiking gear, making it an ideal spot for group camping trips, scout excursions, or family adventures. The colorful, hand-painted beams add a personal touch, showcasing cheerful motifs and inscriptions left behind by previous visitors. Just outside the wiata, a well-maintained fire pit sits ready for evenings of storytelling, warmth, and cooking meals under the open sky. The surrounding area features rocky terrain dotted with resilient bushes and soft heather, further enhancing the shelter’s natural charm. Close by, a bright orange and white lifesaver ring, mounted on a tree, hints at the recreational possibilities offered by the calm waters—swimming, fishing, and canoeing are all within easy reach. As dusk falls, the wiata transforms into a cozy haven—a perfect place to gaze out at the lake as the sky shifts from vibrant blue to deep twilight hues, accompanied only by the gentle sounds of nature. Whether waking up to the calls of birds at sunrise or drifting off to sleep beneath the shelter’s protective roof, guests are immersed in authentic Scandinavian wilderness. This unique spot offers not only protection from the elements but also a profound sense of peace, connection to nature, and timeless memories in one of Sweden’s most enchanting outdoor settings.

properties.name: Övre Glottern
properties.desc:
Walk 2km east from the parking lot towards the sauna. Just follow orange marks. You will first see the sauna, and just around the small hill there's the shelter. You will find a basic outhouse but bring your own toilet paper. No drinking water. It's a very calm place with a beautiful lake where you can take a swim. You need to book the sauna in advance. It's usually fully booked, but they release the dates continuously.

properties.name: DANO 9 - Könnemyren Vindskydd
properties.desc:
To use this shelter you need to buy a Nature Conservation Card. These cards pay for the expansion of campsites, maintenance, wood, supervision, waste containers, nature conservation and compensation to landowners who have granted the use of their land for campsites. Nature Conservation Cards can be purchased online, at the tourist offices in the area or from local canoe rentals. The card costs SEK 60 per person and night. Children under 12 use the service free of charge. Buy Online: https://dalslandnordmarken.se/en/shop/

properties.name: 41008 - Kauksi Bivouac
properties.desc:
The largest RMK recreation site in Estonia, located on the west bank of the Kauksi stream. With its golden sand and light pine forest, Kauksi campsite is among the most highly valued campsites among our visitors. 50 campfire sites, 20 picnic shelters, 3 water wells, 4 dry toilets, observation platform, 3 information boards The maximum number of tents (for 4) allowed is 150 The largest sand dune of the shore of Lake Peipus Camping is free of charge; single entry ticket for a motorized vehicle is 4.00 €. Firewood on sale seasonally at the information point near the campsite entry. There you may also be able to charge your Powerbank

properties.name: Lõhavere Bivouac
properties.desc:
There are two sets of stairs to the hill fort, and 60 m boardwalk leading to an unmarked forest path. The maximum number of tents (for 4) allowed is 10. It is the only ancient hill fort that can be associated with a historical person: Lembitu, the elder of North Sakala, who perished on 21 September 1217 in the Battle of St. Matthew’s Day. Lõhavere hill fort was used at the beginning of the 13th century. The monument of the ancient fight for freedom in memory of the Battle of St. Matthew’s day stands near the hill fort.

properties.name: Nedrema Bivouac & Shelter
properties.desc:
The maximum number of tents on the campfire site (for 4) allowed is 5. It is prohibited to camp in the wooded meadow! Nedrema wooded meadow is one of the largest of its kind in Estonia and Europe. Owing to its large area and well-preserved canopy structure, the Nedrema meadow is among the most distinguished wooded meadows in Estonia. Because of its location between two bogs, Nedrema is quite moist and also has a more versatile terrain than other wooded grasslands in Estonia. 17 species of protected plants, of which 15 are orchids, and 4 rare species of mushrooms have been discovered in Nedrema. The area was listed as protected area in 1991 for the conservation of wooded meadows.

properties.name: CA9 3HF - Greg's Hut
properties.desc:
At an altitude of 2,357 feet (718 m), Greg’s Hut is the highest bothy in England. Located close to Cross Fell, the highest point in the Pennines, this free-to-use cottage is helpfully situated in a remote part of the hills prone to bad weather. There are basic facilities at the bothy and it makes for a good place for a lunch break or emergency overnight stop. For more information, visit: gregshut.org.uk.

Please take your own rubbish away and do not misuse the premises as it is very popular.

properties.name: PH17 2QD - Ben Alder Cottage
properties.desc:
Also known as 'McCook's Cottage', Ben Alder Cottage bothy is a brilliantly remote refuge, nestled between the peak of Ben Alder and Loch Ericht.This medium-sized bothy sleeps up to six people, and has a wood-burning stove, although you'll need to bring your own supply of firewood. This bothy has a rich history, having been used by ghillies, stalkers, fishermen, walkers and poachers over the years, and is also said to be haunted!

properties.name: Jyppyrä Shelter
properties.desc:
There is an extraordinary view of the Hetta area and the surrounding nature from the top of Jyppyrä. You can see the whole Pallas-Ounastunturi Fell chain from a viewpoint near the Campfire Shelter. Hetta Hiihtomaa, Hetta Ski Resort is located near the Campfire Shelter. During winter time it is possible to walk with snowshoes in the area. There is no water source on top of Jyppyrä Hill. It is possible to borrow a coffeepot and a water container from the Fell Lapland Visitor Centre. Visitors must come prepared with warm clothing, food, drink, maps, compass, orienteering skills and matches. Fog or storms can form in just a few hours. Take also your own toilet paper. Metsähallitus follows the principles of litter-free hiking. Visitors are adviced to bring their own waste out of the area. Recycling points can be found at Yllästunturi, Pallastunturi and Fell Lapland Visitor Centre. https://www.nationalparks.fi/jyppyracampfireshelter/

properties.name: 98830 - Lattunavaara Wilderness Hut
properties.desc:
The hut is located on the summit of the wooded hill of Lattunavaara, which is situated on the northern side of the River Ylimmäinen Kivijoki (which empties its waters into River Kemijoki). The hut is not maintained, which means eg. that no firewood is delivered. It's not allowed to make a fire in Lattunavaara open wilderness hut because the chimney is in poor condition and is not safe to use. Making a fire is also prohibited in the old, restored Lattunvaara fire guards hut. Next to the open wilderness hut, there is a hut restored by the National Board of Antiquities. Formerly, both of these huts used to be fire guard's huts. Pets are allowed in the hut, but only with the consent of the other users of the hut. Hikers are asked to bring their own waste to one of the area's recycling points. https://www.nationalparks.fi/lattunavaaraopenwildernesshut

Object Type: Hut

Heating: Yes

properties.name: Pihtijoki Wilderness Hut
properties.desc:
The hut is not maintained, which means eg. that no firewood is delivered to the cabin. Nature Services is looking for an administrator for the cabin. Maintenance includes responsibility for the condition, safety and maintenance of the cabin. Maintenance includes the supply of firewood to the needs of all users, emptying the toilet and waste disposal, and ensuring the overall cleanliness of the site. For more information on cabin maintenance, please contact Olli Vainio, Fieldwork Manager, olli.vainio@metsa.fi. Pets are allowed in the hut, but only with the consent of the other users of the hut. It is also worth taking the first aid kit. Safety on hiking https://www.nationalparks.fi/pihtijokiopenwildernesshut

Object Type: Hut

Fireplace: Yes

Table & Bench: Yes

properties.name: Suttikämppä Wilderness Hut
properties.desc:
The hut is located 4 km west of Tulppio (walk along a path). The hut has been protected and restored by the National Board of Antiquities. It is a log cabin that was built during the logging activities headed by Hugo Richard Sandberg, the "Old Man Samperi", in the early 20th century. In 1993, the buildings that dealt with the mechanised logging activities headed by Sandberg at Tulppio were classified as a nationally significant cultural environment. Pets are allowed in the hut, but only with the consent of the other users of the hut. Hikers are asked to bring their own waste to one of the area's recycling points. https://www.nationalparks.fi/suttikamppaopenwildernesshut

Object Type: Hut

Fireplace: Yes

properties.name: 98830 - Uittipiekka Wilderness Hut
properties.desc:
The wilderness hut is situated on the north side of Uittipiekka Fell, at the head waters of River Sorvorttajoki. Please get acquainted with the instructions and rules of Kemihaara Wilderness area, where the hut is located in. The hut is accessible only by foot or by skiing. Use of motor vehicles in the area is prohibited. There are no marked trails to reach the hut. The area has limited or no mobile phone network coverage. Be prepared that a mobile phone can not be used. Always have a paper map and a compass with you. There is sauna. Uittipiekka hut is located outside marked trails, you need to have orienteering skills to reach it. The shortest way to Uittipiekka in summer time is via Lokka. Drive towards Pihtijoki from Lokka, turn right at Mukkajoenrova and drive until Taltukkaselkä. The distance to the hut from this point is a little over 6 km. Another way to the hut goes via Savukoski. Turn left at Ruuvaoja, drive to the north side of Nivatunturi towards Marjavaara and further to the side of Sorvortantunturi fell. The distance to the hut from this point is a little over 6 km. The roads are forest roads and can be in poor condition. You can reach Uittipiekka in winter time by skiing from Kemihaara direction, where the closest maintained road is. Depending on the route you choose, the distance from Kemihaara to the hut is about 23 km, outside marked trails. There are bins for mixed waste at the hut, but hikers are asked to bring their own waste out of the area. https://www.nationalparks.fi/uittipiekkaopenwildernesshut

Object Type: Hut

Rubbish Bin: Yes

Table & Bench: Yes

Heating: Yes

properties.name: Huttuloma Wilderness Hut
properties.desc:
The wilderness hut is situated 1 km east of Lake Huttujärvi along the Pyhä-Luosto Hiking Trail. In the yard there is a woodshed and outdoor toilet and fireplace Pets are permitted in the hut, but only in case that other visitors agree with it. Huttujärventie road does not have winter maintenance! In the winter time, the easiest way to get to the hut is by skiing (from week 8 onwards by the maintained cross country ski track) or snowshoeing. Hikers are asked to bring their own waste out of the area. https://www.nationalparks.fi/huttulomaopenwildernesshut

properties.name: Härkätunturi Wilderness Hut
properties.desc:
The hut is located close to the border of the municipalities of Salla and Savukoski, in between Härkätunturi Fell and Härkätunturinmuristat Fell The hut is accessible only by foot or by skiing. Use of motor vehicles in the area is prohibited. The area has limited or no mobile phone network coverage. Be prepared that a mobile phone can not be used. Always have a paper map and a compass with you. A stove, an electrical stove and a sauna (used by both those staying at the open wilderness hut and those at the reservable hut) Pets are allowed in the hut, but only with the consent of the other users of the hut. The reservable hut (for 4 persons) is located in the same building How to Get There In summer time, there are several possible starting points from Savukoski direction, close to Tulppio. You can get by car to Tulppionkaitavaara, to the north part of Turjalaiset or Ahmatunturi. The roads are forest roads and can be in poor condition. It is 18 km from Tulppio to Härkätunturi via the UKK-trail. In the winter, the road is maintained to Ainijärvi, 15 km further from Tulppio, where the road ends. From Salla direction, you can get to the hut by walking 23 km north from Tuntsa. You can leave the car at the Tunsta cooking shelter near Tuntsajoki river. Please note that the raod is in very poor condition. There is no marked winter trail going to the hut. https://www.nationalparks.fi/harkatunturiopenwildernesshut

Object Type: Hut

Heating: Yes

Recycling points can be found at Yllästunturi, Pallastunturi and Fell Lapland Visitor Centre. Pets are permitted in the hut, but only in case that other visitors agree with it. properties.name: Marschlerhof
properties.desc:
Previously, a pasture teaching yard stood on this site, which was demolished in GDR times because it was “too close” to the border. Today is a great resting place: two shelter, several table groups, partly covered, trash can and a compost toilet. Camping is officially not allowed, but nice areas would be available. Located directly on the premium trail Hochrhöner and with great views.

properties.name: Borstenplatz
properties.desc:
Thuringia / Rennsteig: The Finnhütte at Bristles Square is built in traditional block construction, inside a wide table and two benches, gravel floor (good support required). Quiet despite close road. The nearest refreshments are: Schmücke (0.9 km), Suhler Hütte (1.1 km), Schneekopf (approx. 2 km). Attention: The opening times on the signposts to the GH Schmücke (day: 10:00 – 21:00) are incorrect!

properties.name: Finsterberghütte
properties.desc:
Thuringia / Alternative route Großer Finsterberg of the Rennsteig = blue "R": The hexagonal hut on the Großer Finsterberg, like many huts in the Thuringian Forest, has a gravel floor, so a good base is necessary here. Accommodates two or three people. The Großer Finsterberg is one of the seven 900-metre mountains on the summit hiking trail.

Object Type: Lean To

Table & Bench: Yes

properties.name: Alte Tränke
properties.desc:

properties.name: Ånnaboda Utsikten
properties.desc:
Very small shelter with beautiful view. Suitable for 2-3 persons. Very exposed to strong wind and the roof is not in the best condition. The fireplace is not placed next to the shelter (safety reasons, i guess, due to strong wind). Probably accessible by road from spring to autumn, but not wintertime because of ski slope crossing the road. Great trails for hiking, skiing and mountainbiking in the whole area. Restaurant, small store, toilet, drinking water etc nearby.

properties.name: Kyreneset campsite
properties.desc:
Accessible only on water. Here you land on a rock, and have to walk a bit in the heather before you reach a large campfire site surrounded by logs. Around this and in the heather there is room for more tents. The place is also suitable for hammocks and wooden tents. Inside the open forest, you are partly exposed to winds from the south and east. https://padleperler.no/2020/05/26/ogge/

Object Type: Bivouac

Fireplace: Yes

properties.name: Landekil Camp
properties.desc:
On this headland a little W of the railway you will find a gapahuk where there is also plenty of space for many tents. Some rubbish here in 2020, so remember to bring rubbish home again! The camp is partly exposed to winds between east, south and west. Here you will also find a toilet. The camp seems widely used. https://padleperler.no/2020/05/26/ogge/

properties.name: Landekil Camp 2

properties.name: Old Train Station Wallendorf
properties.desc:
Kleiner, stabiler, jedoch kalter Steinbau mit festen Betonboden und schmaler Bank. Da Eingang nach Norden ausgerichtet und mit etwas Dachüberstand ausgestattet, dürften sich hier Unwetter halbwegs sicher verbringen lassen, wenn auf Gemütlichkeit verzichtet wird. Auf dem Boden Platz für 3-4 liegende Personen. Außerhalb befindet sich ein Picknicktisch und Mülleimer.

properties.name: Schützhütte Schlabbach
properties.desc:
Frei zugängliche Schutzhütte mit allerlei Einrichtung. Klappstühle, Tische. Schattige Lage in unmittelbahrer Nähe zu zwei Wohnhäusern, die Öffnung jedoch diesen abgewandt. Wenn etwas umgeräumt wird entsteht auf dem Boden ein Schlafplatz für 3-4 Personen. Kein Wasser. Unklar ob öffentlich oder privat, daher unauffällig bleiben und die Nutzung der Feuerstelle vermeiden.

properties.name: Frejas Gård Shelter
properties.desc:
At Frejas Gård there are three shelters with a great view over Jels Nedersø. The three shelters are built in connection with the open-air theater in Jels, and are used by volunteers from Jels Vikingespil during rehearsals and performances for Jels Vikingespiel. This period usually lasts from week 26 to week 29. The rest of the year you can use the three shelters freely. Reservation is not possible. Whoever comes first gets a place to sleep There is also a covered rest area on site. The public toilet facility is (according to information from a local) always open. The water at the sinks is drinking water.

properties.name: Shelter Krämaretorpet
properties.desc:
What a great wind shelter! The toilet house alone is unique. Lovingly furnished with a modern composting toilet, mirrors, books, first aid and even... electric light! There are two firepits, firewood and many benches. Only a few steps away you have a great view over the lake Örsjö. A narrow path through blueberry bushes leads to a small bay. Under a wooden bridge the water from the lake runs off in a stream and is a great source of water. Update 09/2023: the elektric installation was removed (LED-Stripe and switch still installed but cabel were cutted outside and inside) and I didn't found any sign for drinking water you can use the water from the lake but there is no water connection

There are signs at the trailheads but most of the orange trail markers are gone or hardly visible.

properties.name: Rudsklätten
properties.desc:
Small vindskydd with view on Glafsfjorden and next to a hiking trail along several remnants from prehistoric times and several beautiful viewpoints. Unfortunately the way to the vindskydd is forbidden to drive on, except locals, so nearest parking is about 1 km away at the 175. It is a very nice spot to spend an evening at the fire and sleeping should be possible too. It looks very small but I tried and I fit in the length in the vindskydd (I am 1.82m). When I was here a little stock of firewood was present, but it doesn't look very permanent, so I am not sure if this is always the case. The Roof is little bit broken so water runs in the corners. If you are alone it’s ok.

properties.name: Tent spot at a lake
properties.desc:
Around the lake Gyllebo sjö there are many picnic spots where one can easy camp for one night. The water is perfect to refresh oneself after a day of hiking. Camping is allowed for 24 h. Cars up to 2.10 can park next to the lake and the tent. Camper vans are not allowed. But you could probably park next to the official bathing site. It’s supposed to be a fishing lake as well and with apps like iFiske one could get a day license.

properties.name: Feuerwachturm am Farnberg
properties.desc:
The Rennberg near Flaesheim is with 138m above sea level the second highest elevation of the Haard. Near the summit is the Rennberg fire watch tower, which together with the fire watch tower at the Farnberg forms a closed fire watch system in the Haard. It takes 143 steps to reach the viewing platform at 32 meters above the ground. From here you have a great view over the Haard, Flaesheim and the locks at the Wesel-Datteln canal and the southern Münsterland. The best way to reach the fire watch tower is from the Dachsberg hiking parking lot.

Object Type: Tower

properties.name: Pausenplatz am See
properties.desc:
Beautifully situated on the lake. Is probably not an official body, but belongs to a forestry company. Overnight stay went anyway, maybe rather on WE, on weekdays you will be woken up otherwise ;) Since the lake is heavily overgrown with reeds, the entrance to the lake is somewhat more difficult. But have found 200 m westwards a nice bathing area where the entrance was well possible. Schön gelegen am See. Ist wohl keine offizielle Stelle, sondern gehört zu einem Forstbetrieb. Übernachtung ging trotzdem, vielleicht lieber am WE, werktags wird man sonst geweckt ;) Da der See stark von Schilf bewachsen ist, ist der der Einstieg in den See etwas schwieriger. Haben aber 200 m westwärts eine schöne Badestelle gefunden wo der Einstieg gut möglich war.

Object Type: Basic Shelter

properties.name: Cast away
properties.desc:
A rock and wood primitive shelter at a sea coast is a simple yet effective structure that can accommodate up to two people. Constructed using natural materials found in the surrounding environment, such as rocks and pieces of driftwood, the shelter is small in size and situated in close proximity to the water's edge. Despite its rustic appearance, the shelter provides basic protection and a cozy retreat for those seeking an intimate connection with nature. It is not maintained by any "kommun", totally wild. Parking at 59.0334219,18.377906, then 30 min walk west along the gravel road. Next, there's a path to the sea coast (15 min). Then, go left along the coast (10 min).

Object Type: Lean To

properties.name: Zanoni’s Shelter
properties.desc:
Remote shelter 

Condition, not so good.
No wooden flooring but you could pitch a small tent in there or just sit to enjoy the fire and the view.

Fireplace with two benches available. Also benches inside the shelter.
Rubbish bin available

You can only get there through hike trails. 
You can park your car at the picknick site next to the same lake (see the icon at this website) and take a 400m hike to this shelter following the curve of the lake.

Wi-Fi: No. Parking: It is not permitted to drive onto the site, park along the water on Søndre Blvd.

A roofed Gazebo with benches and with a view of the church of the holiest trinity. There are few inhabited homesteads/houses, but I would guess it shouldn't be a problem to stay for a night there if you aren't noisy.

properties.name: Beautiful campsite
properties.desc:
The perfect alternative to the really very ugly Stenshult Vindskydd, which is located next to a sports field. The campsite is absolutely lonely. There is no path leading to the camp site. You should follow the coordinates to find it. The last few hundred meters of the way there are somewhat difficult, but it is quite doable. There is a fireplace and you can find enough wood nearby. There is room to pitch a medium sized tent (or maybe two small ones). You can swim in the lake and the water is drinkable. There are many blueberry bushes growing around the campsite. The place seems to be absolutely unknown. We found it by accident a few years ago and have been there several times. We never saw anyone else there and the fireplace was overgrown with plants each time. So it is rarely used. We probably won't return to Bohusleden anytime soon, so it's time to reveal the secret and show other hikers this great place.

Object Type: Bivouac

properties.name: Steinkreuzhütte
properties.desc:
At the extended square there is a shelter hut made of tree trunks, the Kreuzsteinhütte, built a few years ago by the Fichtelgebirgsverein Speichersdorf; The hut and the site are maintained by the youth group. In the open hut an overview map of the hiking trails. See circular hiking trail 3 from the Tauritzmühle. Am erweiterten Platz steht eine Unterstellhütte aus Baumstämmen, die Kreuzsteinhütte , vor ein paar Jahren vom Fichtelgebirgsverein Speichersdorf errichtet; Hütte und Platz werden von der Jugendgruppe gepflegt. In der offenen Hütte eine Übersichtskarte der Wanderwege. Siehe Rundwanderweg 3 von der Tauritzmühle aus.

properties.name: Shelter Egebaek (private ground)
properties.desc:
The shelter is located on private property. There is a shelter and a covered seating and fire pit. In the house there is a guest room with a small kitchen (socket), bathroom and shower. There is a guestbook and a donation box for the overnight stay. (40 crowns p.p. or more donate) No booking - first come first serve. Firewood is also available for a donation. Der Shelter liegt auf einem Privatgrundstück. Es gibt einen Shelter und eine überdachte Sitz- und Feuerstelle. Im Haus befindet sich ein Gästeraum mit einer kleinen Küche (Steckdose), Bad und Dusche. Es gibt ein Gästebuch und eine Spendendose für die Übernachtung. (40 Kronen p.P. oder mehr spenden) No booking - first come first serve. Feuerholz gibt es ebenfalls gegen eine Spende.

properties.name: Teltschikturm
properties.desc:
43m high observation tower, open on all sides, roofed. Swings in the wind and the railing plays the pan flute. Unofficial bivouac site with very good views. Suitable for hammocks. But you should expect visitors 24/7. On weekends, when the weather is nice, it is a magnet for visitors. At the foot of the tower there are 2 tables with benches and a trash can. It's better not to throw garbage in it, as wild animals like to rummage through the trash can there and then everything is lying around. Best time to stay : Mon-Thu Village is in sight, church bells can be heard

Object Type: Tower

properties.name: offene Hütte an der Hans Klingelhofer Eiche
properties.desc:
Offene Hütte, etwas versteckt im Wald gelegen. Wird wohl seit einigen Jahren nicht mehr benutzt, kann aber als einfacher Schlafplatz gut dienen. Ich würde das Benutzen eines Zeltes oder Tarps in der Nähe der Hütte empfehlen. Sitzmöglichkeiten vorhanden. Auch eine Feuerstelle wurde mal angelegt. Neben der Hütte existiert auch eine Quelle allerdings ist die Wasserentnahme dort schwierig, da es kein Brunnenrohr (siehe Foto) etc. gibt. Mehrere Discounter in ca. 1,5 KM Entfernung am Ortseingang von Bad Hersfeld vorhanden.

Object Type: Lean To

Fireplace: Yes
properties.link1_text: offene Hütte an der Hans Klingelhofer Eiche

properties.name: Auerhahnhütte
properties.desc:

properties.name: Hütte Oberste Liesburg
properties.desc:

Object Type: Bivouac

Fireplace: Yes

properties.name: Schutzhütte Wasserfurche Bavenhausen
properties.desc:
Schutzhütte im Kalletal Bavenhausen. Offene Hütte mit Sitz gelegenheit und Tisch. War auch ein schöner aussenbereich mit Steintisch.weiss aber nicht wie der mittlerweile Aussieht.War länger nicht dort. Es war ein kleiner Steingrill ohne Roste vor Ort. An der Strasse ist eine kleine geflasterte Stelle wo früher ein Schwenkgrill oft Stand. Da könnte man ein kleines Feuer machen. ACHTUNG dann Löscher mit bringen weil Wald nah. Guter Punkt als Start oder end Punkt zum Wandern. Nicht weit ist die Bavenhauser Windmühle. Man kann sehr gut mit dem Fahrzeug dort hin.

Object Type: Lean To

Near Road: Yes

Grill: Yes

Table & Bench: Yes

properties.name: Shelter Brander Bahnhof
properties.desc:
This large shelter offers ample space for multiple people, making it suitable for an overnight stay. The shelter is completely open on one side, providing good ventilation, but this also means that it is fully visible from the outside. Caution is advised when using this shelter as it is located very close to residential buildings, offering little privacy. Advantages: Plenty of space for groups or individuals. Good weather protection due to the covered roof. Easily accessible location. Disadvantages: Lack of privacy due to the open structure and proximity to residential buildings. Noise should be kept to a minimum to avoid disturbing nearby residents. The shelter is situated in an urban area, making it easily accessible, but this also means that wild camping might not be entirely undisturbed here. There's a restaurant across the street where you may can charge your power bank or phone or get tap water for a small tip.

Object Type: Weather Shelter

Near Road: Yes

Parking: Yes

Rubbish Bin: Yes

Electricity: Yes

Drinkable Water: Yes

properties.name: Open shelter
properties.desc:
This open shelter is conveniently located directly along the Vennbahn, making it a great spot for cyclists and hikers to take a break. The sturdy wooden benches and table under a solid roof provide a comfortable place to rest, and the shelter is particularly well-suited for hanging a hammock under the roof structure for overnight stay. However, this shelter is open on all sides, which means that while it provides good cover in calm weather, it is not recommended during heavy rain with strong winds, as it won't offer much protection. The surrounding greenery adds to the serene atmosphere, making it a peaceful spot to relax. This shelter is ideal for short breaks rather than overnight stays, especially in bad weather conditions. It’s a convenient and scenic spot for those passing through on the Vennbahn trail.

Object Type: Basic Shelter

Near Road: Yes

Parking: Yes

Table & Bench: Yes

properties.name: Nice spot by Vänern
properties.desc:
Nice spot, be careful with rocks on the way. Not a good spot for camper, just for SUV or cross country cars (vw bus was also possible). If you want you can drive right by the lake and stay there, or stay at the main area near a little fire place. There is a Factory across the bay, the wind kind of brought the noise to us, but with the beautiful landscape it’s ok. Accessible for 6 Cars or Vans if you squeeze together.

properties.name: Monschau shelter
properties.desc:
This small shelter, designed to resemble a freight car, is located directly at the former Monschau train station along the Vennbahn trail. The structure offers a unique and nostalgic place to rest, with benches and a table inside, making it a convenient spot for cyclists and hikers. The shelter is open on one side, but it still provides a decent amount of protection, making it suitable for an overnight stay, especially for those who don't mind a bit of exposure to the elements.

properties.name: Bivacco Grisetti
properties.desc:
One of few remaining original alpine shelters. A 2 hour steep hike will bring you to 1985m altitude. Here, you will find an amazing original Bivacco with zero amenities. All you can expect to be provided is 9 cramped beds. Make sure you bring your own sleeping bag for hygiene. The views are astonishing, especially at sunset and sunrise. Car can be parked without problems on the side of a service road: 46.343867, 12.098833 PLEASE MAKE SURE YOU LEAVE THE BIVACCO BETTER THAN YOU FOUND IT. Many shelters like this have been removed due to vandalism. this one is a true gem if you don't mind trading some comfort for beautiful views.

Object Type: Hut

properties.name: Wild Camp Spot at the Shores of the Sognefjord
properties.desc:

properties.name: Utleirabura
properties.desc:
Simple shelter with a bench inside. The roof can be quite loud if it rains. You can set up two tents easily if there not too big.

Object Type: Lean To

properties.name: Kleiner Wetterunterstand mit Himmelsliege
properties.desc:
Kleiner Wetterunterstand mit Bänken innen und einer Himmelsliege außen. Unterstand nur bedingt zum Übernachten geeignet, Platz für 1-2 Personen auf dem Boden. Boden bedeckt mit Hackschnitzel. Hängematte nicht möglich. Außerhalb ebene Flächen die Zelten ermöglichen. Schöne Aussicht, ruhige Lage.

Object Type: Basic Shelter

properties.name: Ederstede Wetterunterstand
properties.desc:
Kleiner Wetterunterstand mit Bänken. Bedingt zur Übernachtung geeignet, Hängematte nicht möglich. Außerhalb befindet sich eine Sitzgruppe. Fußläufiger Zugang zur Eder, dort gute Bade- und Waschgelegenheit. Ruhige Örtlichkeit. Grillen und Feuer sind explizit verboten.

Object Type: Basic Shelter

Table & Bench: Yes

properties.name: Ederstede Wetterunterstand

properties.name: Eichenlaubhütte
properties.desc:
Halboffener Unterstand. Dach scheint dicht zu sein. Bänke innen ringsum, mittig ein beweglicher Tisch. Boden schmutzig, Platz für 2-3 Personen. Mülleimer wird nicht geleert, daher Müll bitte mitnehmen. Eine kurze (<3Meter) Hängematte diagonal zwischen den Ständern möglich. Längere, wenn man einseitig mit langen Seil an an einem der Bäume anbindet. Landstraße gegenüber hörbar, angrenzend eine Kuhweide und in Sichtweite ein Hochstand.

Object Type: Basic Shelter

Fireplace: Yes

properties.name: Schutzhütte
properties.desc:
Hexagonaler Grundriss. Dichtes Dach. Seitlich montierte Bänke. Mittige Platz für 4-5 Personen. Bodenbelag aus staubigen Schotter. Hängematte möglich. Aussicht auf Weide, ruhige Lage.

Object Type: Lean To

properties.name: Blockhaus
properties.desc:
Massive Blockbohlenbauweise auf Stelzen, großzügige Terrasse, im Inneren wandseitige Bänke, mittags Freifläche. Rundum guter Witterungsschutz. Innen Liegefläche für 4-5 Personen, auf der Terrasse für 2-3 Personen. Kurze Hängematte (<3Meter) möglich.

Manche von ihnen, wie der Grünschenkel oder die Löffelente, sind nur auf der Durchreise. Andere, wie der Graureiher und Kormoran, halten sich ständig hier auf. Infos zu den Arten findest du am Turm.

properties.name: Vallbokvarnströmmen, Nyköpingsån.
properties.desc:
Situated on elevated ground overlooking the Vallbokvarnströmmen, this site comprises three shelters arranged in a U-shape. The structures are low in height, providing excellent protection in adverse weather conditions. Together, they offer sufficient space to accommodate more than twenty people comfortably. A fire pit is positioned at the center of the three shelters, while an outdoor toilet and waste bin are located along the track leading out to the road. This resting place is ideally situated for those paddling along the Nyköping River (Nyköpingsån).

properties.name: Campsite between wooden beehives and a quarry
properties.desc:
There is plenty room for a tent. It is easily accessible by foot or bike. There is wind protection from the west and north. When I was there in October 2025, the wooden beehives were empty. There is also a small fireplace. A mountain bike park is also located on the west side of the campsite. At night, you can hear the mooing of the cows from a nearby cowshed. The place is not visible from outside, someone would need to walk there for you to be seen.

properties.name: Rentnertempel
properties.desc:
Pavillon des örtlichen Rentnerclubs in schattiger Lage an einer Weggabelung.m Wer Gesellschaft, neugierige Fragen und die Geschichten der Alten nicht scheut, findet hier eine bequeme und trockene Unterkunft. Witterungsgeschützt können hier zwei nicht zu große Personen unter dem Tisch auf weichen Holzchips übernachten. Es gibt reichlich Sitzkissen und Polster vor Ort. Verlasse den Ort so, dass es deine Großeltern glücklich machen würde.

properties.name: Shelter in blueberry field
properties.desc:
Easy to access by bike. Cycleway very close (around 50 m). Lake not accessable because of private property. No drinking water and electricity supply. But very nice and calm place to stay. Shelter has no lying surface (bare ground), but fireplace and tables with benches. Blueberries everywhere around. Nice forest! This point is set manually a day after visit. GPS point will match not 100 %, but will be very close. Shelter can be also found in Komoot (link below). https://www.komoot.de/highlight/1667709?ref=ahd

Object Type: Weather Shelter

Fireplace: Yes

Grill: Yes

Table & Bench: Yes

properties.name: Fosby
properties.desc:
This place is available from land and water. My first visit was with a kayak. My second was with a car. Kids friendly location by Aremarksjøen lake with rocky and a sandy beach. An older lean-to that is not wind proof since some wall planks have loosened. The roof seems OK. Roof beams and supporting coloumns seems sturdy enough for a hammock. A firepit with a chimney in the lean-to. 100m from parking. Ample area for pitching a tent. Bring your own firewood or collect at location.

properties.desc:

properties.desc:
